organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

companyResolutionResult.description:
Get SAT is the leading provider of Micronized Satellite Terminals.

The InterFlat is the latest breakthrough in antenna panel technology,

providing the world’s highest efficiency performance in the smallest package.

Combining the InterFlat with our state-of-the-art control and tracking mechanism,

creates the most powerful and fully contained satellite terminal system.

The InterFlat is a leap forward in capability and SWaP,

enabling us to provide truly portable, full duplex, wide-band satellite communications.

Get SAT customers include top tier defense, commercial and technology companies.

The Get SAT team is composed of industry-leading experts with multi-disciplinary skills,

dedicated to providing an exceptional, agile and affordable customer experience.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Afeka Tel Aviv Academic College of Engineering
Welcome to the Afeka Tel Aviv Academic College of Engineering!

Located at the heart of the Israeli high-tech industry in Tel Aviv, the Afeka Academic College of Engineering has positioned itself as one of the leading institutions for engineering studies in Israel. Afeka offers a strong and cutting-edge academic program that produces highly skilled and professional engineers. The exceptional level of training, coupled with Afeka's strong ties to the industry, paves the way for Afeka graduates to position themselves at the forefront of the Israeli and International engineering community.

The Afeka Academic College of Engineering is accredited by the Israel Council for Higher Education and offers B.Sc. degrees in the following fields of engineering:

Medical Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Mechanical Engineering
Industrial Engineering and Management
Software Engineering
Computer Science
Data Science


M.Sc. Program in:
Medical Engineering
Intelligent Systems
Systems Engineering
Energy and Power Systems Engineering
Industrial engineering and management

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

מטרת אוניברסיטת רייכמן (לשעבר הבינתחומי) היא להכשיר את מנהיגות העתיד של מדינת ישראל, באמצעות השכלה בינתחומית, המשלבת ידע תיאורטי מעמיק עם הכשרה מעשית, ובהנחיית סגל מרצים וחוקרים מהאוניברסיטאות המובילות בעולם ובישראל. גישתנו היא הקפדה על מצוינות במחקר ובהוראה, לצד דגש על אחריות חברתית ומעורבות בקהילה. מאז הקמתו, הצליח הבינתחומי הרצליה להיות חלוץ בתחומים רבים ולהוביל מהפכה במערכת ההשכלה הגבוהה בישראל, שאת עקבותיה ניתן לראות כיום במוסדות אקדמיים אחרים.

עקבו אחרינו בכל הרשתות החברתיות >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

The program combines students from the fields of Business, Data Analytics, Entrepreneurship, Economics, Accounting, and Psychology.
Students in the program are "tweezer-picked" and represent the elite, in terms of scholastic aptitude and personal achievements.

Throughout the program, students engage with prominent instructors from various sectors, both in Israel and globally. They follow a distinctive curriculum, featuring diverse and innovative content, along with a robust extracurricular component that facilitates direct interaction with influential figures in the business realm.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In an era where technology and artificial intelligence are reshaping every aspect of life, the demand for professionals who can navigate the complex interplay of data science, computer science, and management is surging. Our Business and Data Analytics track is at the forefront of this revolution, offering a curriculum that merges cutting-edge data science and computer science methodologies with core business principles.

This track is designed to equip future leaders with the analytical skills and technical knowledge necessary to thrive in today's data-driven business environment. By integrating subjects like programming, artificial intelligence, machine learning, finance, marketing, and entrepreneurship, we prepare our students not just to meet but to exceed the evolving demands of the 21st-century workplace.

With a strong emphasis on practical application, students engage in problem-solving to gain hands-on experience throughout their studies. This pragmatic approach ensures our graduates leave with a comprehensive skill set that aligns with the current and future needs of the business world.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
HackerU is a leading Techcelerator dedicated to training the next generation of the high-tech industry. 
We provide comprehensive services for individuals seeking to make a career change and enter the Israeli high-tech sector. Our all-in-one solution includes personalized guidance, screening, training, and job placement assistance. With over 30 years of experience, we excel at identifying candidates' potential, matching them with suitable fields, and providing top-notch professional training.
As a part of TechDX group, we are spearheading the Tech Education revolution globally, delivering high-quality training programs in various institutions such as universities, government bodies, armies, and police forces. We offer training in over 14 different fields, including programming, information and cyber security, digital marketing, graphic design, gaming, and 3D animation. Our flexible morning/evening study formats range from 4 to 14 months, and prior experience in the field is not necessary for acceptance. We believe in equalizing opportunities through education and strive to make high-tech training accessible for all individuals who possess the motivation and desire to integrate into the industry.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

resumePosition.details:
• Designed and implemented daily scheduled bulk jobs in Hadoop framework, Improving data processing efficiency for business-critical operations.
• Developed an internal web service product (using React and Rest API) to streamline the routine management of on-call team members, automating daily task allocations and reducing manual effort.
• Gained deep expertise in databases integration and principles, working with systems like Hive/Impala (Hadoop framework), T-SQL (MS-SQL), PL-SQL (Oracle SQL) and Elasticsearch.
• Well versed in team-based development using Git, performing code reviews (CR) and working with CI/CD pipelines for efficient and reliable software delivery.
• Worked extensively with both Microsoft servers and linux servers, ensuring smooth deployment and system maintenance in varied environments.
• Authored comprehensive mentorship documentation to onboard new team members, accelerating their ramp-up time.
• Then the documentation was contributed to 8200 data-science course, enhancing the training program while also grading their assignments on the contributed material specifically, and their Python exercises generally.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

companyResolutionResult.description:
Dream is a pioneering AI cybersecurity company delivering revolutionary defense through artificial intelligence. Our proprietary AI platform creates a unified security system safeguarding assets against existing and emerging generative cyber threats. Dream's advanced AI automates discovery, calculates risks, performs real-time threat detection, and plans an automated response. With a core focus on the "unknowns," our AI transforms data into clear threat narratives and actionable defense strategies. 

Dream's AI cybersecurity platform represents a paradigm shift in cyber defense, employing a novel, multi-layered approach across all organizational networks in real-time. At the core of our solution is Dream's proprietary Cyber Language Model, a groundbreaking innovation that provides real-time, contextualized intelligence for comprehensive, actionable insights into any cyber-related query or threat scenario.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ביה"ס הריאלי העברי בחיפה הנו מוסד חינוכי עצמאי (וחל"ץ). תלמידינו, המתקבלים לביה"ס בתהליך של מיון ובחירה, נהנים ממסלול חינוך רצוף מגן ועד י"ב, ומתחנכים לאור חזון ביה"ס: "להיות מוסד חינוכי איכותי, מוביל ומתווה דרך, המכוון את תלמידיו להיות בני אדם, מעורבים ומשפיעים בחברה הישראלית".

מאז היווסדו בשנת תרע"ד (1913) על רקע "מלחמת השפות" והדרישה לייסד בית ספר ריאלי שיהיה כולו עברי – התפתח ביה"ס לאורם של מאורעות מרכזיים ביישוב היהודי ובמדינה תוך שהוא משמש חלוץ ומצפן בעשייה החינוכית בישראל. בין המפעלים החינוכיים ש"נולדו" אצלנו ואומצו על ידי משרד החינוך ניתן למנות את ייסוד הצופים, הגדנ"ע, "השירות הלאומי", ה"מחויבות האישית", ה"חיבור השנתי", "חברת התלמידים", מקצועות האזרחות, המזרחנות ועוד.

כיום מונה ביה"ס כ- 4,000 תלמידים בשישה סניפים וכ- 500 אנשי סגל, כ- 80 מתוכם בוגרי ביה"ס. לכל סניף בביה"ס מאפיין ייחודי משלו, תחת תפיסה חינוכית כוללת:

מתחם הדר - מדעים ואומנויות
מרכז הכרמל (מטו"ס) - מדע, טבע וסביבה
מתחם אחוזה - מנהיגות ותקשורת
החטיבה העליונה בבית בירם המציעה מעל 50 צירופי מקצועות
אשכול גנים ירוק - בקמפוס בית בירם

התלמידים שלנו מגיעים לביה"ס מרחבי העיר חיפה ומהפריפריה, מנהריה בצפון דרך יקנעם במזרח ועד זיכרון יעקב בדרו�...

The website reaches the vast majority of the shipper market, with an average of 3,360 unique visitors per day. This makes it a significant source of news and information for the Israeli logistics and transportation community.
The magazine and website are supported by advertising from major service providers, such as FedEx, UPS, DHL, El Al, CAL, Maersk, ZIM, COSCO, China Shipping, Hapag-Lloyd, Hamburg Sud, Safmarine, MSC, ONE, Swissport, UTi, YangMing, the Israeli Port Authority, Ashdod Port, Haifa Port and Eilat port.
The Chief Editor of the magazine is Pninit Berman, and the Reporter and Photographer is Chanoch Winnykamien.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

currentPositions.description:
MentorAI is an innovative AI-driven platform revolutionizing the creator economy by enabling influencers to monetize their knowledge through simulated, personalized mentoring sessions. The platform analyzes YouTube content to create digital mentors, providing users with 24/7 guidance and creators with a scalable product. 

As a Backend Developer and AI Specialist, I lead the system's architecture from POC to a fully scalable production environment. I engineered a robust backend using Python and Django on AWS, integrating VectorDB and RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) for accurate context retrieval. My work includes implementing asynchronous processing with Celery and Redis to handle heavy workloads like video transcription and data analysis, packaged within Docker containers for seamless deployment. 

I successfully launched the MVP, scaling the system to support hundreds of active users and improving answer quality using 'Agentic RAG'. This phase included optimizing API response times, establishing a CI/CD pipeline, and implementing a comprehensive logging system. Seeing real users interact with the product and iterating based on their feedback has been a major professional milestone.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

• Defined data communication with executive management to enhance global markets prioritization and strategy decisions.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem is Israel's first university, formed in 1918 and opened in 1925. Today, there are over 23,000 students enrolled at the University, including undergraduates, master's degree students, and doctoral candidates.

The university has four campuses: Mount Scopus, The Edmond J. Safra (Givat Ram) Campus, and Ein Kerem (in Jerusalem), and Rehovot.

Hebrew University Alumni are invited to visit https://alumni.huji.ac.il

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Welcome to the Jerusalem College of Technology (JCT), a leading institution where technological innovation meets traditional Judaic studies in the heart of Jerusalem. At JCT, we provide world-class education in technology, health sciences and business, coupled with a rich Judaic studies program that prepares students for leadership in a globalized world.

About Us:

At JCT, we are committed to excellence in education. Our programs in engineering, computer science, health sciences, business, and more are designed to equip students with cutting-edge technical skills and a strong ethical foundation. Our unique blend of modern technology and Jewish values offers a distinctive educational experience.

Why choose JCT?

Top-Tier Education: Our esteemed faculty deliver high-quality instruction in various fields, preparing our students for their future careers.

Comprehensive Judaic Studies: Students don’t have to choose between academic excellence and spiritual growth. Our Judaic studies program is designed to enhance both. Our top-level shiurim and rich religious environment allow students to deepen their spiritual and ethical development while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Strategic Location: Situated in the historic and vibrant city of Jerusalem, JCT provides a stimulating academic environment enriched by cultural, spiritual and intellectual opportunities.

Supportive Community: We offer a collaborative community where students, faculty, and alumni support each other. Our focus on mentorship and career development ensures that students have the resources they need to excel.

Connect with us to learn more about our programs, events, and opportunities.

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Jerusalem College of Technology

companyResolutionResult.description:
HRMoney | Data. Insights. People.

At HRMoney, we believe that the strength of any organization lies in its ability to understand, connect, and manage its people - through data.

Our cloud-based platform (built on AWS) complies with the highest security standards and unifies all your workforce data - compensation, attendance, engagement surveys, HRIS, BI, and ERP - into one smart system that delivers real-time, actionable insights.

What do we empower organizations to do?

🔹 Make data-driven decisions – Predict attrition, optimize rewards, mitigate risks
🔹 Increase employee connection & retention – Foster loyalty and engagement
🔹 Boost productivity – Empower people who understand their value
🔹 Optimize labor costs – Pay accurately, reduce turnover, cut onboarding waste
🔹 Enhance transparency & control – Prevent legal exposure, build trust, strengthen brand

We’re not just an HR tool - we’re an insight engine that bridges the gap between data and people, helping organizations grow through their people, and lead their people through data.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges
